B. He has to go digging to find them.
C. He trades textbooks for them.
D. He inherited them from early Magdalenians.
E. He crafts them himself to teach his classes.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Believe it or not, from a thrift shop. 5 bucks.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Art
evidence_dialogues.utterance: You lucky dog! Me, I gotta go digging for this kinda stuff.

reasoning_steps.inference: John obtained his from a thrift shop.
Art contrasts John's luck with his own method, which is 'digging'.

qa.question: What explicit common professional status does Art claim he shares with John?
option: A. They are both published authors.
B. They are both teachers.
C. They are both thrift shop owners.
D. They are both biologists.
E. They are both anthropologists.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Art
evidence_dialogues.utterance: John we're teachers, we answer silly questions all the time.

reasoning_steps.inference: Art explicitly pairs himself and John under the label 'we're teachers'.

qa.question: What is the specific price of the item Art envies John for finding?
option: A. 14,000 bucks
B. 10 bucks
C. 5 bucks
D. 190 bucks
E. 140 bucks

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Believe it or not, from a thrift shop. 5 bucks.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Art
evidence_dialogues.utterance: You lucky dog! Me, I gotta go digging for this kinda stuff.

reasoning_steps.inference: John states the price is 5 bucks.
Art expresses envy over this specific acquisition.

session_1.text: Scene 4: The Burin

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: John, we're all sorry to see you go. Truly.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Okay. Now we're done with that, what do we do for the rest of the afternoon?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Anyone got a good topic?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Dan picks up a carved flint tool from a box.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Like this, maybe? Heh.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: What is that?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's a burin. Of a parrot beak. Inclined chisel point...probably early Magdalenian.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: May I see that?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Sure.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: (hands it over)

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: (examines it carefully)

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Yes indeed, that's what it is.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: What's a...burin?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: A burin's a flint tool for grooving wood and bone, antlers especially, to make spear and harpoon points. Magdalenians weren't noted for flint work, so this is a very nice specimen.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (smirking)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Okay. What's a "Magdalenian"?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: A later Cro-Magnon, without getting technical.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's the final culture of the upper-Paleolithic.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: If stones could speak, eh, Art?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: So where'd you get that, John?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Believe it or not, from a thrift shop. 5 bucks.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: You lucky dog! Me, I gotta go digging for this kinda stuff.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Can I, uh...?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Yeah.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Huh...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (motioning to Edith then handing her the burin)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (surveying the room)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: ...Maybe...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I'm glad you did this.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Did what?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: You mean, come over?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: ...Maybe?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Definitely.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Gee, thanks!

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, so are we(?).

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: So are we! We couldn't let you just run off.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (taking the burin from Edith to put back into the box)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Thanks.
Scene 5a: The Set-up

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: John, what is up, huh?! Are you running from the cops? We won't turn you in!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (pacing)

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Yeah come on, out with it. You're among friends.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Snoopy friends.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Forget it.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: You are creating the mystery here. Obviously you have something you'd like to say. Say it!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Well, maybe I...

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Harry, stop.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: There is something I'm tempted to tell you, I think. I've never done this before and I wonder how it'll pan out.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: ...I wonder if I could ask you a silly question.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: John we're teachers, we answer silly questions all the time.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Hey!

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: (elbowing Art)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: What if a man from the upper Paleolithic...survived until the present day?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: ...Wh-what d'ya mean "survived"? Never died?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yes. What would he be like?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Arnold Schwarzenegger.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: A few smirks.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's an interesting idea. What, are you working on a science-fiction story?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Say I am. What would he be like?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Pretty tired.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Everyone chuckles.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, seriously, as Art's book title suggests, he might be like any of us.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Dan! A caveman?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, there's no anatomical difference between, say, a Cro-Magnon and us.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Except that as a rule, we've grown taller.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: What's the selective advantage of height?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Better to see predators in tall grass, my dear.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Actually, tall and skinny radiates heat more effectively in warmer climates.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: And as for Neanderthals, that strain's still with us, of course.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Arnold Schwarzenegger!

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: But...he'd be a caveman.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: No, he wouldn't. John's hypothetical man would've lived through a hundred and forty centuries...

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Yeah, roughly.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: ...And changed with every one of them. I mean, assuming normal intelligence. Well, we think men of the upper Paleolithic were as intelligent as we are. They just didn't know as much.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: John's man would have learned as the race learned. In fact, if he had an inquiring mind, his knowledge might be...astonishing.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: (pointing at John)

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: If you do write that, let me have a look at it.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: I'm sure you'll make some anthropological boners.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: It's a deal.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: What would keep him alive?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: What does the biologist say?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Cigarettes. And ice-cream.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Everyone scoffs and chuckles.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Alright, alright, I'll play, I'll play.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Alright, um, in science fiction terms, I would say...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: ...Perfect cellular regeneration caused by circulating Mesenchymal stem cells originating from bone marrow. Especially in the vital organs. Actually, the human body appears designed to live about a hundred and ninety years. Most of us just die...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (motions to his glass)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: ...of slow poisoning.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (smiles and takes a sip)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Maybe he did something right. Something everybody else in history had done wrong.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: What, like eat the food, drink the water and breathe the air?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: ...Prior to modern times, those were pristine.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We've extended our lifespan in a world that's, uh... not fit to live in.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: (sighs)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Y'know, it could happen! Yeah! The pancreas turns over cells every twenty-four hours, the stomach lining in three days, the entire body in seven years, but the process falters. Waste accumulates, eventually proves fatal to function. Now if a quirk in his immune system led to perfect detox, perfect renewal, then yeah. He could, uh... duck decay.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Mm, that's a secret we'd all love to have.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Would you really want to do that? ...Live 14,000 years?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Well, if I could stay healthy and I didn't age, I mean, why not?

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Yeah. What a chance to learn.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Is anyone hungry?

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: (Heads off-stage to the kitchen)
Scene 5b: The Revelation

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Y'know, the more I think about it, yeah, it's possible. Anything is possible, right? After all, one century's magic, another century's science. They thought Columbus was a nut-case, right? Pasteur? Copernicus?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Aristarchus long before that.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (Nonchalantly, as he gets up to pour himself another drink)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I had a chance to sail with Columbus, only I'm not the adventurous type.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: What?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Music starts...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I was pretty sure the Earth was round, but at that point, I still thought he might fall off an edge some place...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Incredulous, confused reactions.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: (Bemused, laughing nervously)

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Look around, John. We just did.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: I suppose there's a joke in there somewhere, but I don't get it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: There's nothing to get.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Music builds...

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: What are we talking about?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: We were just talking about a caveman...who survives until the present time.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: As you said, what a chance to learn, once I learned to learn.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Did you start the whiskey before we got here?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Pretend it's science fiction. Figure it out.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Okay, a- (heh heh) -very old Cro-Magnon living until the- present.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Music crescendos...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (slowly leans forward and suddenly GRUNTS LOUDLY like a caveman)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Everyone jumps, startled, and then laughs.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Oh!
Scene 6a: The First Lifetime

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: (entering)

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: What?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: John just confided that he's fourteen thousand years old.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Oh, John, you don't look a day over nine hundred!

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: (laughing)

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Okay!

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Alright Spock, I'll play your little game. What do you want? What's the punch line?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Every ten years or so, when people start to notice I don't age, I move on.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Pause.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: ...That's very good, that's very quick John. I wanna read that story when you're done.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: You want more?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Yeah, by all means. This is great! Alright, now... (chuckles) ...So you think you're a- a- uh- Cro-Magnon.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Well, I didn't learn it in school. That's my best guess. Based on archaeological data, maps, anthropological research. Since Mesopotamia, I've got the last...four thousand years straight.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Well you're ahead of most people, so please, go on.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Well you know the background stuff, so I'll make it brief. In what I call my first lifetime, I aged to about thirty-five. What you see. I ended up leading my group. They saw me as magical. I didn't even have to fight for it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Then, fear came. And they chased me away. They thought that I was...stealing their lives away to stay young.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: The prehistoric origin of the vampire myth. That is good!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: First thousand years, I didn't know up from sideways.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: How do you know the first thousand years?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: An informed guess, based on what I've learned in my memories.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Most people can scarcely remember their childhood, but you have memories of that time?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Like yours. Selective. Y'know, the high points, the low points, traumas. They stick in the mind forever. Put down at three or thirty-five, you still feel a twinge.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Go on.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I kept getting chased because I wouldn't die, so I got the hang of joining new groups I found. I also got the idea of periodically moving on. We were semi-nomadic, of course, following the weather and the game we hunted. The first two- thousand years were cold. We learned it was warmer at lower elevations. Late glacial period, I assume.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: What was the terrain like?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Mountainous. Vast plains to the West-

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: West - something you learned in school.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Towards the setting sun. I suspect I saw the British Isles from what is now the French coast.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Huge mountains on the other side of an enormous deep valley that was shadowed by the setting sun. This is before they were separated from the continent by rising seas, as the glaciers melted.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: That happened?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Yes, the end of the Pleistocene. So far what he says fits.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Oh, yeah, into any textbook.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: And that's where I found it. How can I have knowledgeable recall if I didn't have knowledge?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: It's all retrospective. All I can do is integrate my recollections with modern findings.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Caveman, you gonna hit me over the head with a club and drag me into the bedroom?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: You'd be more fun conscious.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Oh, John.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Let me get this straight. We're not talking about, uh, reincarnation. You're not saying that you remember, whatever the hell it would be, 200 separate lifetimes, dying and being born again and yada yada?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: One lifetime.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Some lifetime.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (laughs)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Wow! Maybe there is something to this reincarnation thing. You're supposed to come back again and again, learn and learn, and somehow, John, you just managed to bypass all the other bodies.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, what's the point?

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: What about oceans?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Didn't see them till much later.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: So how would you know an ocean from a lake?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Big waves. Something else I can only surmise in retrospect.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Were you curious about where it all came from?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: We would look up at the sky and wonder. "There's gotta be some big guys up there. What else made all this down here?"

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: At first I thought there was, uh, something wrong with me - maybe I was a bad guy for not dying. Then I began to wonder if I was cursed, or perhaps blessed. Then I thought maybe I had a mission.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Do you still think you do? God works in mysterious ways.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I think I just happened.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Phone rings, aside.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (laughs)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Wow.
Scene 6b: Ellie's Midterm

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (picking up the phone, set slightly away from stage)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Hello?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yes, Ellie?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: What's wrong?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (calls out)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Sandy?

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Coming.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Yeah?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Do we have Ellie's midterm here?

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Yeah, sorry. I picked it up with the periodicals.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Got it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: No, you're worried about your parents? Don't- don't worry. You passed, C+.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (Changes grade on paper)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Take care of yourself.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Good kid. What does a pre-Med need with history?
